text: Zone 2 training should preferably be performed at intensities located immediately below the first lactate or ventilatory threshold.
Zone 2 training can be effectively performed through continuous, variable, or interval-type sessions.
Zone 2 training produces a broad range of both central and peripheral physiological adaptations.
The physiological adaptations from zone 2 training are not unique to that exact intensity and can also be induced by sessions performed at slightly higher or lower intensities.
A large proportion of total endurance training volume should be performed at zone 2 (low) intensity for enhancing endurance performance.
